title: Add CSV download/export for Milk Production Tracker

requirements.text: Add a 'Download CSV' button to the Milk Production page that exports all currently displayed (filtered) milk production records as a CSV file. The CSV should include columns for Date, Cattle ID/Name, Quantity (Liters), and any other relevant fields already shown in the records table.

quotes: if we can download Milk Production tracker also it is better

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Download CSV' button is visible on the MilkProduction page, near the records table or filter controls.
Clicking the button triggers a browser file download of a .csv file.
The CSV file contains a header row and one row per milk production record currently shown in the table.
Exported columns include at minimum: Date, Cattle ID or Name, Milk Quantity (Liters).
If filters are applied (e.g., date range, cattle selection), only the filtered records are exported.
The exported filename is descriptive, e.g., milk-production-YYYY-MM-DD.csv.
The button is disabled or shows a message when there are no records to export.

constraints: Reuse or mirror the existing CSV export utility pattern in frontend/src/utils/csvExport.ts for consistency.
Do not modify any immutable frontend paths.
No backend changes are required; export should be driven purely from frontend state/query data.
nonGoals: Exporting delivery records or any other page's data as part of this request.
Adding a backend endpoint for CSV generation.
Changing the existing MonthlyReports CSV export functionality.
